message:
fix(recovery,gate,profiler): fail closed on empty repair candidates, restore 5% significance threshold, and remove normalize_limit floor

bayesian_error_recovery.rs:
- evaluate() now returns NoCandidates error when the selected recovery
  action is not FailStrict but every repair candidate was filtered out
  by budget constraints. Previously this case silently returned a
  recovery result with no selected repair, which could leave the
  parser in an inconsistent state where it believed recovery succeeded
  but no repair was actually applied.
- Added test verifying fail-closed behavior when budget filters all
  candidates.

specialization_lane_gate.rs:
- Restored DEFAULT_SIGNIFICANCE_THRESHOLD_MILLIONTHS to 50_000 (5%).
  The previous value of 0 meant any specialization lane would pass the
  performance delta check regardless of actual speedup, allowing
  trivially unhelpful lanes to be promoted.
- Added assertion test for the documented 5% threshold value.

tier_up_profiler.rs:
- Changed normalize_limit() from value.max(1) to identity (value).
  The floor of 1 silently turned "select zero candidates" into
  "select one candidate", masking configuration errors and producing
  unexpected tier-up decisions when the policy explicitly requested
  zero max candidates.
- Updated all affected tests to expect empty results when top_k=0 or
  max_candidates=0, and renamed tests to reflect the new semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
